flashcards.definition: Intracellular fluid; ~2/3 of total body water; K⁺ is the major cation.
Extracellular fluid (~1/3 of TBW); includes plasma and interstitial fluid; Na⁺ is the major cation.
Abnormally high blood sodium; usually from water deficit → dehydration.
Low blood potassium; can cause dangerous cardiac arrhythmias.
Arterial pH < 7.35 due to HCO₃⁻ loss or acid gain; kidneys excrete H⁺, retain HCO₃⁻.
